meta.title: Self-Ordering Kiosk for Hospitality — Order Terminal & Self-Service | Gastro Master
meta.description: Self-ordering kiosk from Gastro Master: guests order and pay directly at the terminal — no queue, no counter stress. Multilingual, 24/7, automatic upselling, fully integrated into the POS system. For fast food restaurants, bakeries, food courts and franchises.

hero.headline: Self-Ordering Kiosk — End of the Queue
hero.subline: The terminal takes over the counter. Guests choose, configure and pay directly on screen — multilingual, with active upselling and no waiting time. Orders land in real time in the POS and kitchen. Your staff works instead of taking orders.

problem.title: The Problem at the Classic Counter
points: During rush hour everything backs up at the counter — guests wait 8–12 minutes, many turn away and don't come back.
Staff shortages in hospitality: even one missing till operator noticeably reduces revenue per shift.
Manual order taking takes 90–120 seconds per guest — time that's directly missing during lunch or rush hour.
Upselling only works when staff have the time and inclination — in practice, larger portions, sides and drinks are regularly missed.

solution.title: The Solution: Self-Ordering Kiosk
points: Guests order themselves on the touchscreen — completely done in 60–90 seconds, with clear images, quantities and add-on options.
Payment directly at the terminal: card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, cash optional. No additional step at the till.
Active upselling in the order flow: Large portion of chips? Drink with that? +20–30% average basket, all without staff.
Order lands instantly in POS and kitchen, with order number for the pick-up screen. No double entry, no misunderstandings.

features.headline: Everything You Need for Self-Ordering That Really Relieves the Pressure
features.sub: Six features that turn the terminal into a fully-fledged ordering and payment channel.

items.title: Intuitive Touch Operation
items.description: Large buttons, clear images, German and international visual language. Even elderly guests and children get through without help — no explanation needed.

items.title: Automatic Upselling
items.description: For each item you store recommendations — size upgrade, sides, drinks, desserts. Appears automatically in the order flow and demonstrably increases the basket by 20–30%.

items.title: Multilingual Menu
items.description: German and English — guest chooses their language at the start. More languages coming in the next releases.

items.title: All Payment Methods Integrated
items.description: Debit card, Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, Google Pay — directly at the terminal. All payment methods are automatically handled through Epit Pay. Cash module upgradeable on request.

items.title: 24/7 Operation Possible
items.description: For petrol station bistros, hotel lobbies or unmanned self-service: the terminal takes orders around the clock, even when nobody is at the counter.

items.title: Live Sync with POS & Kitchen
items.description: Every order lands instantly in the cloud POS and on the kitchen monitor. Pick-up screen calls the order number. No double entry, no lost orders.

useCases.headline: How Restaurateurs Use the Self-Ordering Kiosk
useCases.sub: Three real scenarios from our customer base — from fast food to food courts.

items.title: Fast Food Chain with Lunchtime Rush
items.story: A burger concept in Berlin-Kreuzberg replaced two till positions with four self-ordering kiosks. Average waiting time fell from 8 to under 4 minutes — lunchtime revenue rose by 28%, without extra staff.

items.title: Bakery with Takeaway
items.story: A bakery outlet at Hamburg Central Station installed two kiosks next to the counter. Guests with a quick takeaway need order at the terminal, staff can focus on consultation and filled rolls — +35% orders per hour.

items.title: Food Court with Multiple Concepts
items.story: An operator of several food stations in a Munich shopping centre placed a kiosk terminal in front of every stand. Guests order at the terminal and receive an order number right away. A large pick-up screen displays the order numbers as soon as the orders are ready — staff call out, guests collect. The counter became a pure handover station, labour costs per shift reduced by 1.5 FTE.

stats.label: shorter wait per guest during rush hour
higher average basket through active upselling
orders without staff at the counter

testimonial.quote: We regularly had 15 people queuing at lunch — many left again. Since the four kiosks we handle the same rush in half the time, and the average basket has risen by €4.80 because the terminal suggests a drink and a dessert to everyone. For my counter crew, the biggest stress-killer of recent years.
testimonial.author: Tarek B.

pricing.headline: Pricing & Availability
pricing.note: POS add-on for fast food restaurants, bakeries, food courts and franchise concepts. The price depends on form factor (counter, freestanding, wall-mounted), number of terminals, card reader integration and optional cash module. In a free consultation we put together the right setup for your business — including hardware, installation, configuration and connection to your cloud POS.

internalLinks.headline: Works Perfectly With
internalLinks.intro: The self-ordering kiosk unfolds its full potential as part of the Gastro Master ecosystem — from the order at the terminal to handover to the guest.

items.title: POS System
items.description: The heart of it all. Every kiosk order lands automatically in the cloud POS, on the kitchen monitor and on the pick-up screen — with order number, items and special requests.

items.title: Restaurant Solution
items.description: Complete package of POS, self-ordering, display system and kitchen monitor — specifically for fast food restaurants, quick service concepts and businesses with high on-site throughput.

faq.headline: Frequently Asked Questions about the Self-Ordering Kiosk
faq.sub: Answers to the questions we receive most often from fast food restaurants, bakeries, food courts and franchise businesses.

items.q: What does a self-ordering kiosk cost for my business?
items.a: The price depends on form factor (counter, freestanding, wall-mounted), number of terminals and chosen modules (card reader, optional cash acceptance). We don't offer the kiosk as a list price — instead, after a free consultation we put together a specific quote. Payment can also be arranged in flexible instalments. In most fast food restaurants and bakeries a kiosk pays for itself within 6–12 months.

items.q: Which businesses benefit most from a self-ordering kiosk?
items.a: Particularly businesses with high order volume and short dwell times: fast food concepts, burger and pizza chains, bakeries with a lunch menu, cafes with takeaway, food courts, franchise systems, petrol station bistros, hotel lobbies. Rule of thumb: from 80–100 orders per day or regular peak times with queuing, the first kiosk pays for itself quickly.

items.q: How many kiosks do I need for my business?
items.a: Rule of thumb: one kiosk replaces roughly one till operator at peak times. For a fast food restaurant with 60–80 seats we recommend 2–3 terminals, for a bakery outlet with lunch trade 1–2 terminals, for a food court stand with pick-up 1 terminal per concept. The exact number is determined in consultation after reviewing your daily revenue distribution.

Three main variants: counter (stand-up terminal for counter or bar, compact), freestanding (floor-standing column with large display, ideal for entrance area or centre island) and wall-mounted (space-saving, perfect for narrow sales areas like bakeries). All form factors come with a card reader, cash module optional.

items.q: Which payment methods does the terminal support?
items.a: Debit card (girocard), Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Apple Pay and Google Pay are integrated as standard. All payment methods are automatically handled through Epit Pay. A cash module with change dispensing is optionally available — useful for businesses with a high cash proportion.

items.q: How does upselling at the kiosk work exactly?
items.a: For each item you can store matching recommendations in the backoffice — larger chips with the burger, a drink to go with it, a dessert at the end. The kiosk displays these suggestions automatically in the order flow. Industry average increases the basket by 20–30% — without staff having to actively sell. You retain full control over what's offered when.

items.q: Which languages does the kiosk menu support?
items.a: Currently we support German and English. Additional languages will be added in the coming releases. The guest selects their language at the start with a tap.

items.q: Is the kiosk connected to my POS system?
items.a: If you already use a Gastro Master POS system, the kiosk is immediately ready. Orders from the terminal land automatically in the cloud POS, on the kitchen monitor and on the pick-up screen — with order number, items, special requests and payment status. No separate setup, no third-party interface, no developers needed.

items.q: What happens during an internet or power outage?
items.a: The terminal requires a stable internet connection. We therefore recommend a LAN cable connection to avoid short-term WiFi issues. An optional WiFi function is also possible. During a power outage, the display restarts automatically after recovery and reloads the last state from the cloud.
